title: Resolve LCI mesh proxy resource ambiguity
description: Discovered during project-LICHEN-py4c.1.2 review. spec/11-lci.md discovery advertises </mesh>;rt="proxy", but section 17.5.5 says clients can address mesh nodes directly and no special proxy resource is required. Python currently exposes /proxy when a mesh client is configured. Decide whether native LCI should use direct IPv6 only, /mesh, /proxy, or a compatibility alias set; update discovery/link-format/spec/code and Python TUI probing rules. No hardware required.
notes: Evidence from project-LICHEN-py4c.1.2 review: spec/11-lci.md discovery example lists </mesh>;rt="proxy", but section 17.5.5 says the client can reach any mesh node by direct CoAP address and no special proxy resource is required. python/src/lichen/coap/resources.py defines ProxyResource and registers it at /proxy when mesh_client is configured. The Python TUI screen map now treats direct IPv6 CoAP as the preferred conceptual model and /mesh or /proxy as optional discovered capabilities only until this Bead resolves the contract.
